answer:
The preemphasis filter should not increase the bandwidth, meaning the mean-square frequency deviation after preemphasis must not exceed that without preemphasis. This condition leads to an inequality involving the integral of the product of the preemphasis response and the modulating signal’s spectral density. Solving that integral with the given \( G(f) \) and \( f_1 \ll f_m \) yields the bound \( k^2 \leq \frac{\pi}{f_1(2f_m - \pi f_1)} \), which matches option I.

\boxed{I}
